classification.reasoning: The patch introduces new utility methods for type checking in OpenSearchTypeFactory, adds a new class BinnableField to encapsulate validation logic, and modifies multiple binning handler classes to use this new validation approach. It also removes redundant validation code and adds comprehensive integration and unit tests. These changes involve structural additions and refactoring across multiple files, including new class creation and method extraction, indicating structural changes rather than simple string replacements or formatting fixes. The localization evidence shows most hunks found via exact or fuzzy matching, supporting a moderate to high confidence in classification as TYPE_III. The patch is not a deep refactor or redesign, so TYPE_IV or TYPE_V are not appropriate. The complexity is consistent across hunks, so MIXED is not applicable. The estimated token budget is relatively high due to the number of files and complexity of changes.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added two new static methods to check if a RelDataType is numeric or time-based, supporting both standard SQL types and OpenSearch user-defined types.
hunk_descriptions.where_in_code: OpenSearchTypeFactory class, new static methods isNumericType and isTimeBasedType
hunk_descriptions.intent: To provide utility methods for identifying numeric and time-based types, enabling consistent type checking that includes OpenSearch UDTs.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Removed unused imports related to type checking and simplified class-level comment.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To clean up imports and clarify the class purpose focusing on bin-specific field operations.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Removed the isTimeBasedField method that checked time-based types and UDTs.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To remove redundant or unused time-based type checking logic, possibly replaced by centralized checks elsewhere.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added a new class BinnableField that encapsulates a validated field supporting binning, ensuring the field is either numeric or time-based at construction.
hunk_descriptions.where_in_code: BinnableField class with constructor and requiresNumericBinning method
hunk_descriptions.intent: To encode field validation into the type system, preventing misuse of binning operations on invalid field types and avoiding repeated validation.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added imports for BinFieldValidator and BinnableField.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To enable usage of field validation and encapsulation utilities in count bin handler.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Inserted creation of a validated BinnableField instance from the field expression and name before processing bins parameter.
hunk_descriptions.where_in_code: CountBinHandler class, method handling CountBin node
hunk_descriptions.intent: To ensure the field used in count binning is validated as numeric or time-based before proceeding with bin count logic.
Code cleanup by removing unnecessary imports.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added import of BinnableField to the DefaultBinHandler.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To support usage of the BinnableField class for validated field handling in default binning.

hunk_descriptions.where_in_code: DefaultBinHandler class, method handling DefaultBin node
hunk_descriptions.intent: To centralize and enforce field validation before applying time-based binning logic.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added imports for BinFieldValidator and BinnableField.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To enable field validation and encapsulation in minspan bin handler.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Inserted creation of a validated BinnableField and added a check that minspan binning only supports numeric fields, throwing an exception otherwise.

hunk_descriptions.intent: To enforce that minspan binning is applied only to numeric fields, preventing invalid usage on time-based fields.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added imports for BinFieldValidator and BinnableField.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To support validated field handling in range bin handler.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Inserted creation of a validated BinnableField and added a check that range binning requires numeric fields, throwing an exception otherwise.

hunk_descriptions.intent: To ensure range binning is only applied to numeric fields, enforcing correct usage.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Replaced direct time-based type check with creation of a validated BinnableField and used its isTimeBased property for branching.
hunk_descriptions.where_in_code: SpanBinHandler class, method handling SpanBin node
hunk_descriptions.intent: To centralize field validation and ensure binning logic branches correctly based on validated field type.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added a comment clarifying that the field is already validated as numeric before analyzing the span value.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To document that numeric validation is guaranteed before processing span value, improving code clarity.
